purpose: Structured taxonomy for credit cycle risk assessment that separates structural vulnerabilities from triggers and amplification mechanisms to enable targeted hedging
components: Vulnerabilities: structural conditions creating fragility (covenant-lite, EBITDA addbacks, private credit opacity, CLO concentration)
Triggers: events that activate vulnerabilities (fiscal consolidation, maturity wall, basis trade unwind, rate shock)
Amplification mechanisms: channels through which initial stress propagates (CLO OC tests to forced selling to private credit markdown to BDC gates to insurance RBC to broad repricing)
6-step non-traditional contagion channel mapping
current_assessment: Multiple vulnerabilities identified and catalogued; triggers are uncertain in timing; amplification channels involve 5-6 interruptible steps. Hedging should target amplification mechanisms rather than unpredictable triggers using the three-layer framework (CDX HY options + MOVE exposure + CLO equity avoidance).
limitations: Framework catalogs risk-amplifying changes more thoroughly than risk-dampening improvements
Trigger timing remains inherently unpredictable
Amplification chains have multiple interruption points reducing probability of full transmission
No systematic way to net vulnerabilities against structural improvements (bank capital 2-3x, central bank backstops)

purpose: Accounting identity framework explaining how fiscal deficits sustain corporate profits and suppress the credit cycle turn, and why all historical timing analogues are unreliable
components: Corporate profits approximately equal investment + fiscal deficit - household savings - current account deficit
At 6-7% GDP deficit, fiscal channel sustains ~$1.4-1.6T annual corporate profits
Profit support suppresses defaults by estimated 100-150bp of spread compression
Self-referential instability extension: deficit creates Treasury supply that reprices term premium and eventually reverses the suppression
current_assessment: The fiscal buffer is real and currently the dominant credit cycle suppressant. No historical analogue exists for this deficit magnitude alongside a mature credit cycle. Whether the buffer merely delays or fundamentally alters the cycle is the central unresolved question.
limitations: Cannot predict when fiscal impulse will contract
Whether buffer merely delays or fundamentally alters the cycle is unresolved
The 2pp GDP consolidation trigger threshold is contested
Self-referential instability loop has never been triggered by fiscal causes

purpose: Calibrate true leverage and default probabilities by adjusting reported metrics for EBITDA addbacks and covenant-lite structures using structural credit modeling
components: Reported leverage input (4.0-4.5x for median B-rated)
EBITDA addback adjustment (25-40% addback realization discount applied to reported EBITDA)
Asset volatility assumption calibration
True leverage output (5.5-7.5x after adjustment)
Default probability recalibration (from ~3.5% to ~8-14% 5-year cumulative)
S&P LCD cross-validation (75-85% realization rates)
current_assessment: The most robust quantitative finding in the synthesis. Direction is unambiguous; magnitude depends on addback realization rates and assumed asset volatility. Even conservative estimates are economically significant.
limitations: Exact magnitude depends on addback realization rates which range 75-85%
Asset volatility assumptions create additional uncertainty
Model calibration may not capture private credit dynamics
Static analysis that does not account for dynamic balance sheet management

purpose: Systematic framework for combining multiple historical credit cycle episodes with similarity scoring to generate conditional forecasts with explicit uncertainty bounds
components: Historical episode identification and documentation across credit cycles
Multi-dimensional similarity scoring (leverage, rates, fiscal stance, structural features)
Weighted composite construction based on similarity scores
Conditional probability estimation with explicit uncertainty ranges
Yield curve inversion duration as severity predictor (22 months current, r=0.65 with recession severity across n=7)
Demographic compression adjustment for neutral credit spreads across decades
current_assessment: More disciplined than ad hoc analogies but fundamentally limited by the unprecedented fiscal deficit context. No prior credit stress episode operated with deficits above 3.5%, making all historical analogues unreliable for timing.
limitations: Small sample sizes (n=5-7 episodes) limit statistical inference
Current fiscal context is unprecedented, degrading all historical analogues
Similarity scoring requires subjective weighting
Assumes structural stationarity that may not hold given post-GFC changes

purpose: Diagnose crowding in credit factor exposures, assess illusory diversification, and identify when credit risk premia are inadequately compensating for true risk profile
components: PCA decomposition: first PC explains >65% of variance across HY, leveraged loans, CLO equity
Dispersion analysis: compressed dispersion as crowding signal
Higher-moment analysis: skewness (-1.2 to -1.8) and kurtosis (4-8) of credit returns
Implementation friction adjustment: Sharpe ratio reduction from 0.5-0.7 backtested to 0.25-0.35 realized
Cross-asset inconsistency detection: term premium vs credit premium divergence
current_assessment: Credit sub-sector diversification is currently illusory with returns dominated by a single factor. Crowded positions will unwind simultaneously during stress. Market pricing undercompensates for true risk profile when accounting for higher moments and implementation costs.
limitations: Does not predict timing of crowding unwind
Backtested factor adjustments may not generalize to current market structure
Higher-moment estimates are sensitive to sample period
Dispersion threshold for signaling peak crowding is not precisely defined

purpose: Credit cycle hedging framework targeting amplification mechanisms rather than unpredictable triggers, designed to be actionable regardless of trigger timing
components: Layer 1: CDX HY options for direct credit spread protection
Layer 2: MOVE index exposure for rates volatility hedge covering basis trade and Treasury supply risk
Layer 3: CLO equity avoidance to remove exposure to the most leveraged and opaque part of the credit stack
current_assessment: Addresses identified amplification channels. CLO equity avoidance is passive risk reduction; CDX HY options and MOVE exposure provide active hedging. Internal inconsistency noted in the long credit vol / short equity vol variant where correlation spikes cause the funding leg to generate losses when the primary leg pays off.
limitations: CDX HY options have negative carry during extended benign periods
MOVE exposure may not fully hedge non-Treasury rate channels
Does not address private credit mark-to-model risk directly
Long credit vol / short equity vol variant has internal inconsistency under own stress scenario
